etymology.description: 卩 (kneeling person) + 䇂 (sickle-like tool) -> administer; regulate; punish
etymology.interpretation: The character 辟(bì, 'distinguish') originally combined 卩 (a kneeling person) and 䇂 (a sickle-like tool) to convey the act of administration or regulation, possibly hinting at the implementation of justice or punishment. Over time, 〇 was added but its role is debated, with some suggesting a phonetic or decorative function. The earlier form's focus on law enforcement leads directly to the modern sense of differentiation through regulation.

examples.chinese: 科学家们试图辟谣(科學家們試圖辟謠)。
examples.english: The scientists attempted to debunk the rumors.

examples.chinese: 他能够清楚地辟出真相(他能夠清楚地辟出真相)。
examples.pinyin: Tā nénggòu qīngchǔ de bì chū zhēnxiàng.
examples.english: He can clearly distinguish the truth.
